text: fat is 11g. saturated fat is a major pressure point. for dairy, the fat side can either add richness or drag the whole thing down.
carbs is 24g. the carb side gets messy pretty quickly. for a dairy food, protein and practicality have to carry the case.
protein is 3.5g. essential amino acids score is where things start to fall off. useful protein can rescue a lot of weaker traits here.
vitamin b12 at 6% DV, vitamin a at 8% DV stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
calcium at 13% DV, zinc at 3% DV, magnesium at 2% DV stand out most. useful, but still pretty limited.

the upsides first. high enjoyment factor because a real-world reason people eat it, even if the category fit is weak. still retains some calcium because not nutritionally empty, just weak compared with stronger dairy foods. still clearly dairy-based because that matters for fairness when picking anchors.

on the weaker side. sugar load is a major dairy-category problem because this pushes it away from what strong dairy foods are supposed to deliver. saturated fat is a major drawback because a second major strike against the food in this category. protein usefulness is weak for dairy because that makes it a poor fit compared with stronger dairy anchors like yogurt.
D tier. Best only when the taste or format matters more than getting the strongest dairy profile.
